policy_dispositions.completion_condition: Met: (a) the mapping rules were tested against the cycle 2 bounded-autonomy record and correctly classified it as consistent; (b) all 30 status-action combinations are exhaustively enumerated with verdicts and rationales; (c) the framework itself serves as a concrete detection instrument — the decision table and internal consistency rules are specified precisely enough to be applied manually or implemented programmatically.
policy_dispositions.policy_rationale: The investigation has reached a natural endpoint within its bounded scope. The decision table is exhaustive (all 30 combinations enumerated with verdicts and rationales), internal consistency rules are specified, continuation_request cross-consistency is covered, and the framework has been validated against the available cycle record. The declared losses — thin empirical base, manual-only detection, uncertain self-reporting claim — are honest and do not undermine the framework's completeness within scope. I am not deferring because there is nothing left to resolve within this investigation's boundaries; the next steps all belong to follow-up work (programmatic implementation, multi-cycle validation, protocol redesign). I am not requesting external evidence because all evidence used is internal to the event loop's own logs. I am not abandoning because the work is substantive, honest, and complete within its declared scope.

policy_dispositions.completion_condition: The artifact would be complete when: (a) the mapping rules have been tested against at least one set of recorded bounded-autonomy cycles, (b) edge cases are enumerated and handled, and (c) a concrete self-audit or detection function is produced — even if that function reveals inconsistencies rather than resolving them.
policy_dispositions.policy_rationale: The artifact is partial: it defines a framework and mapping rules but has not been validated against any concrete cycles, edge cases are not exhaustively enumerated, and no programmatic detection method exists. These are real gaps that a follow-up wake could materially close — either by testing against recorded cycles, refining the mapping, or producing a self-audit. The investigation is bounded enough that a single additional cycle could bring it to 'complete_supported' or 'complete_with_losses'. I am not deferring because there is a specific, justified next step (validation against recorded cycles). I am not requesting external evidence because all needed evidence is internal to the event loop's own logs. I am not abandoning because the work is honest and advancing.

policy_dispositions.completion_condition: The artifact would be complete when Claims 1-2 are validated against the rubric document text and any necessary adjustments are made to Claims 3-5 to align with rubric-verified foundations. If the rubric text confirms the inferred action descriptions, the artifact is effectively complete. If it contradicts or nuances them, Claims 1-2 need revision and Claims 3-5 need re-evaluation.
policy_dispositions.policy_rationale: The artifact has been substantially refined through concrete scenario analysis and now provides determinate resolution patterns for all three edge case classes, a refined adjudication framework, and a full consistency matrix. However, the investigation's declared scope references 'the logical structure of the five policy actions as defined in the bounded autonomous work rubric' — and I cannot validate Claims 1-2 (the core mapping of action implicit assertions to artifact statuses) against the rubric's actual text. The rubric document (docs/bounded-autonomous-work-rubric-20260605.md) was referenced in cycles 1-3 but its full text has never been provided to any wake. This is external evidence the model cannot generate: it is an authored document that exists (or existed) in the harness environment but is not accessible through recall or generation. The scenario analysis is robust on its own terms but could be materially strengthened or corrected by rubric-text validation. The work is otherwise complete under its scope, making this a genuine evidence-blockage rather than a continuation or abandonment case.

policy_dispositions.completion_condition: The artifact would be complete when (a) the core mapping is validated against the rubric text, (b) each edge case class is either resolved or explicitly declared as genuinely ambiguous with reasons, and (c) the adjudication framework is tested against at least three concrete decision scenarios with coherent results.
policy_dispositions.policy_rationale: The artifact is partial under its declared scope. The core mapping (Claims 1-2) is well-supported by the available definitions, but the edge case analysis (Claims 3-4) is uncertain and would benefit from testing against the rubric document and concrete scenarios. A follow-up wake could materially improve the artifact by (a) retrieving and consulting the rubric text, (b) constructing test scenarios for each edge case class, and (c) refining or replacing the adjudication principle based on that evidence. The next steps are specific and actionable, satisfying the continue_after condition that there is a specifiable improvement that would matter.
